bonus_fact: Mother Flawless Sabrina organized drag pageants in the 1960s, helping create safe spaces for self-expression.

title: Roy Ayers: Jazzy Vibes and Sunny Tunes
story: Roy Ayers brought sunshine to the world through music. Born in Los Angeles in 1940, he became a master of the vibraphone. Ayers blended jazz, funk, and world music to create unique sounds. His band Ubiquity and hit song 'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' made people groove. On September 10, we celebrate this talented composer and record producer.
activity_question: What instrument was Roy Ayers known for playing?
bonus_fact: The vibraphone is like a xylophone with metal bars and spinning discs that create a vibrating sound.

title: A Champion for People with Disabilities
story: Jean Vanier, a kind-hearted Canadian, came into the world in Geneva, Switzerland in 1928. Born on September 10, he grew up to become a philosopher who cared deeply about others. Vanier dedicated his life to helping people with disabilities. He believed everyone deserves love and respect, no matter their abilities.

title: A Trailblazing Seminole Leader
story: Alice Brown Davis broke barriers in 1922. Born in Park Hill, Indian Territory on September 10, 1852, she became the first female Principal Chief of the Seminole Tribe of Oklahoma. Davis led her people for over a decade, serving until 1935. Her groundbreaking role paved the way for future generations of Native American women in leadership.
activity_question: What historic role did Alice Brown Davis hold?
activity_choices: First female Principal Chief of the Seminole Tribe of Oklahoma
First Native American woman in Congress
First Seminole to write a book
activity_answer: First female Principal Chief of the Seminole Tribe of Oklahoma

title: Stargazer's Birth: Unraveling Saturn's Secrets
story: James Edward Keeler, a curious boy who grew up to unlock cosmic mysteries, was born in 1857. His passion for the stars led him to become a famous astronomer. title: A Life Spanning Three Centuries
story: Maria de Jesus entered the world in Olival, Portugal, in 1893. Little did anyone know she'd become a supercentenarian! Born when horse-drawn carriages ruled the streets, Maria lived to see the age of smartphones. She witnessed incredible changes, from the first airplane flight to humans walking on the moon. On September 10, 2008, she celebrated her 115th birthday, making her the oldest person in Portuguese history.

title: Gibraltar's Overwhelming Choice
story: Tiny Gibraltar made a big decision! In 1967, this rocky peninsula held a vote about its future. The people had to choose: stay with Britain or join Spain? When the votes were counted on September 10, the result was clear. An enormous majority picked Britain, with only a handful preferring Spain. It was a landslide victory for keeping things as they were.
activity_question: What did the people of Gibraltar vote about?
activity_choices: Whether to stay with Britain or join Spain
Whether to build a new airport
activity_answer: Whether to stay with Britain or join Spain

title: Space Shuttle's Epic Journey Home
story: Soaring through the skies, the Space Shuttle Discovery made its triumphant return in 1984. After an incredible mission, the massive spacecraft didn't head straight to Florida. Instead, it took a detour, landing at Altus Air Force Base in Oklahoma on September 10. From there, the shuttle continued its journey back to its launch site at Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
